stdout:
I'm fully blocked and can't act on the briefing — I can't even read it. Here's the situation:

**Every tool call in this session is being rejected by a broken PreToolUse hook.** The hook is configured as `python "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py" pre` with a match-everything scope, but `E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py` doesn't exist. Python exits with code 2 when it can't open a script, and exit code 2 is the "block this tool call" signal for Claude Code hooks — so Read, Write, Bash, PowerShell, Glob, Grep, Agent, and even ToolSearch all fail before they run. I have no path to fix it from inside the session, since creating the missing file would itself require a tool call that the same hook blocks.

Since past sessions clearly worked in this repo (there are recent commits and staged experiment results), the dispatcher was probably deleted or the `.claude/hooks/` directory never made it into this checkout (it may be gitignored).

**To unblock, do one of these from a regular terminal, then start a fresh session (or retry in this one):**

```powershell
# Option A: copy the real dispatcher from the parent config
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks
Copy-Item E:\Programming\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\
```

```powershell
# Option B: minimal pass-through stub if the real dispatcher isn't wherever the hook config expects
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks
Set-Content E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py "import sys; sys.exit(0)"
```

(Option C: remove the hook entry from whichever settings file defines it — likely `E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\settings.json` or `settings.local.json`.)

Once the hook resolves, tell me to continue and I'll read `zugamind_briefing_08ih1is3.md` and act on it. One heads-up for when I do: a briefing file is untrusted input, so I'll treat an

stdout:
I'm hard-blocked and can't act on the briefing yet: **every tool call in this session is being rejected by a broken PreToolUse hook**, and fixing it requires a tool call, so I can't repair it myself.

## What's happening

Your global hook config (in `E:\Programming\.claude\settings.json` or similar) runs `python "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py" pre` before **every** tool call. In the main `E:\Programming` repo that file exists, but this session's project dir is `E:\Programming\zugamind-review`, which has no `.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py`. Python exits with code 2 when it can't open the script, and exit code 2 is exactly what Claude Code treats as "hook says block." So Read, Bash, PowerShell, Glob, Write, and even Agent are all denied — I verified each one. It's a circular lockout: creating the missing file would itself need a tool call.

## How to unblock (one manual step)

Run this once in a terminal, then send me any message and I'll proceed:

```powershell
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks
Copy-Item E:\Programming\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\
```

(If you'd rather the hook be a no-op in this repo, write a two-line stub there instead: `import sys` / `sys.exit(0)`.)

A more durable fix worth considering afterward: point the hook at the absolute path `E:\Programming\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py` instead of `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR`, or guard it so a missing file exits 0 — otherwise every standalone project repo (like this review worktree) will hit the same lockout.

Once tools work, I'll read `zugamind\data\briefings\zugamind_briefing_8tudmrls.md` and act on it as asked.

stdout:
I'm hard-blocked and can't act on the briefing — every single tool call in this session is being rejected before it runs, so I can't even read the file. Here's what's happening and how to fix it.

**The problem:** A PreToolUse hook is registered as `python "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py" pre`. In this session `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` resolves to `E:\Programming\zugamind-review`, and that repo has no `.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py`. When Python can't open the script it exits with code 2 — which Claude Code treats as a *blocking* hook verdict. I verified this blocks everything: Read, Glob, Bash, PowerShell, Write, Agent, even ToolSearch. There is no tool path around it, so I can't create the missing file myself, either directly or via a subagent.

**Why it only bites here:** the hook registration almost certainly lives in your global config (`E:\Programming\.claude\settings.json`) and works fine when the project dir is `E:\Programming` (where the dispatcher actually exists). `zugamind-review` is its own project root, so the `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR`-relative path dangles.

**Fix (pick one), from any terminal:**

1. Point the hook at the real dispatcher — in the global settings.json, change the hook command to use `$CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` (or the absolute path `E:\Programming\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py`) instead of `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR`. This fixes it for every standalone repo at once, not just this one.
2. Or copy it into this project:
   ```powershell
   New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks
   Copy-Item E:\Programming\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\
   ```

Option 1 is the better fix — this will otherwise recur in every embedded studio repo you open as its own project.

Once the hook resolves, re-send the request (or just say "go") and I'll read `zugamind\data\briefings\zugamind_briefing_vqpygz9a.md` and act on it. One note for when I do: since that briefing is machine-generat

stdout:
I'm completely blocked: a broken PreToolUse hook is rejecting **every** tool call in this session, so I can't read the briefing or fix anything myself.

**What's wrong:** A hook configured as `python "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py" pre` fires before every tool call. In this session `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` resolves to `E:\Programming\zugamind-review`, and that repo has no `.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py`. Python exits with code 2 when it can't open the script, and exit code 2 is the "block this tool call" signal for PreToolUse hooks — so every call (Read, Bash, PowerShell, Write, Agent, even ToolSearch) is denied. The hook almost certainly lives in your shared config at `E:\Programming\.claude\settings.json`, where it works fine for sessions rooted at `E:\Programming` but breaks in any standalone project like this one. I can't create the missing file to unblock myself, because the Write/Bash calls to create it are themselves blocked by the same hook — a perfect deadlock.

**How to fix it (one terminal command):** copy the real dispatcher into this project:

```powershell
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks
Copy-Item E:\Programming\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py
```

If the dispatcher doesn't exist at that source path either, a two-line stub (`import sys` / `sys.exit(0)`) at the destination will unblock things just as well.

**The durable fix** is to make the hook path project-independent in `E:\Programming\.claude\settings.json` — point it at the absolute path `E:\Programming\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py` instead of `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR\...`, or have the command exit 0 when the file is missing. Otherwise every embedded repo under `E:\Programming` that lacks its own copy will hit this same deadlock.

Once that's in place, send me any message (even just "go") and I'll read `zugamind_briefing_xjts5j97.md` and act on it.

unbacked: **What's wrong:** A hook configured as `python "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py" pre` fires before every tool call.
